mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of rate-decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and reduced corporate earnings growth, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above the 5.0 percent threshold during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal income and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer spending and construction investment, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of triggered months, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with p greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which translates to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days during economic expansions.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive returns during expansion phases, with a hit rate below 45% over a 5-year walk-forward validation period.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product confirms sustained macroeconomic expansion, supporting higher corporate profit expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 50% of quarters following positive GDPC1 growth, or the out-of-sample correlation coefficient falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 245.0, accelerating inflation erodes real consumer purchasing power and forces tighter monetary policy expectations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in more than 65% of months where CPIAUCSL crosses the 245.0 threshold, invalidating the inflation penalty effect.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with a stable or declining federal funds rate creates a soft-landing environment that boosts equity valuations, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal generates negative returns in more than 50% of observed months, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts signals deteriorating real estate investment and consumer confidence, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during late-cycle phases.
failure_condition: The signal predicts positive returns in more than 55% of late-cycle months, or the directional accuracy drops below 40% in out-of-sample walk-forward testing.
